description_long: This is a two-story brick house with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in reddish-brown shingles. The front facade features a prominent triangular gable with large, floor-to-ceiling windows that provide ample natural light to the upper level. The exterior walls are constructed from reddish-brown brick, and the house includes multiple rectangular windows arranged in a mostly symmetrical pattern. There is a covered porch area on one side, supported by simple columns, and a main entry door located on the opposite side of the front. Several metal chimneys and vent pipes protrude from the roof, indicating the presence of fireplaces or heating systems. The rear of the house features a large, glass-enclosed area, likely a sunroom or living space, with a wide balcony or deck beneath.
description: A two-story brick house with a steep gable roof, large front windows, and a covered porch. The design features symmetrical window placement and a prominent glass-enclosed rear area.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main gable with large triangular windows, a covered porch on the left, and two rectangular windows on the right.
description_view_1: The left side view displays two vertically aligned windows and the steep pitch of the roof, with visible chimneys and vent pipes.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals a mix of windows and doors, a secondary gable, and the entry door, with a small window to the right and a covered porch to the left.
description_view_3: The right side view highlights the expansive glass wall and balcony/deck area beneath, with the roof extending over the open space.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with large front gable windows
attribute_entryway_design: Main entry with a simple door, covered porch supported by columns, and minimal decorative elements
